title: remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group has redundant DELETE group_members when count=0
description: store.rs remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group (~line 1021): after deleting the specific (group_id, pub_id) row at line 1027 and confirming remaining==0 at line 1032, the code issues a second DELETE FROM group_members WHERE group_id=?1 at line 1038 before deleting the group row. Since remaining==0 was just confirmed within the transaction, this second DELETE always affects 0 rows (it is dead code). More importantly, if this logic were ever adjusted (e.g. to a DEFERRED transaction that misses a concurrent insert), the second DELETE would silently purge a newly added member. The fix is to remove the redundant DELETE at line 1038-1041; the first DELETE at line 1027 is sufficient, and the group DELETE at line 1042 is the correct next step.
